event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.proof: The projection validates the exact twelve-node target bound, reserves one exact arena, constructs info plus eleven direct leaves through accepted XML value and tree contracts, and retains typed indexed errors.
The eleven direct leaves remain version through v6service_port in accepted LSLC-001H order, and LSLC-001C represents delimiter-sensitive opaque values without adding semantic parsing.
The accepted LSLC-001O source remains borrowed, unchanged, and reusable while the result owns distinct validated name and character-data allocations.

The local info-rooted element tree adds no provider, acquisition, declaration, observed whitespace, self-closing policy, static/desc merge, complete document, clocks, host reads, identity generation, endpoint semantics, transport, runtime, or Manifold authority.
Cargo files, dependencies, feature lock, effect union, accepted evidence, oracle drivers, external-source boundary, devices, and runtime surfaces remain unchanged and inert.
Public instructions keep accepted data, XML representation, provider, complete-document, runtime, and authority layers distinct; shared skills are reviewed and workflow remains device-free.

document.commit_policy: Commit the borrowed volatile XML element projection separately from providers, static/description merging, declarations, observed whitespace or self-closing policy, complete documents, protocol, transport, adapters, runtime activation, features, devices, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the borrowed volatile XML projection, exact order, allocation/error behavior, and strict provider/complete-document/runtime boundary.
Describe the new local representation layer without conflating it with accepted data, acquisition, full documents, or runtime support.
Review whether the explicit data-to-representation projection adds reusable guidance; retain unchanged if current artifact-role rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether this source-only Rusty LSL representation changes repo-family routing; retain unchanged if current public and authority boundaries cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, project or feature locks, accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, generated protocol material, native libraries, or implementation-source inputs.

Provider traits or selection, acquisition, clocks, time calculation, host or environment inspection, random or generated identifiers, address or port parsing, socket ownership, networking, discovery, inlet, outlet, buffering, recovery, FFI, adapters, or runtime activation.

Static field or desc composition, merging multiple info roots, XML declaration, inter-element whitespace, self-closing tags, attributes, namespaces, schemas, parsing, decoding, canonicalization, endpoint bytes, or complete-document compatibility.

Interpreting opaque version, creation, identity, session, host, address, or port values as current runtime facts, protocol, wire, identity, security, recovery, routing, permission, admission, or authority evidence.
Oracle execution, network access, external implementation-source inspection, devices, Quest, Hostess, platform lifecycle, product policy, or operational/ecosystem compatibility claims.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, or product-policy authority.
document.objective: Borrow one accepted LSLC-001O volatile field set and project its eleven opaque values into one bounded local XmlElementTree with an info root and direct leaves in accepted LSLC-001H order, reusing LSLC-001B through E representation contracts while preserving the source unchanged and adding no provider acquisition, complete-document spelling, clock or host inspection, generated identity, address or port interpretation, networking, runtime activation, or Manifold authority.
outputs: A dependency-free borrowed projection into one twelve-node info-rooted XmlElementTree with eleven direct leaves in accepted volatile role order.
Separate explicit XML text, represented-byte, and tree limits with fixed node-indexed delegated errors and deterministic validation/allocation precedence.
Exact preservation of the borrowed LSLC-001O source and separate owned copies for accepted XML names and represented character data.
Focused evidence covering empty, Unicode, delimiter-sensitive, and deliberately non-semantic opaque values without promoting the result to a complete document or provider behavior.
